title: Digital arrest scams - the new fraud everyone should know about
p1: You receive a video call. A person in uniform appears, calm but firm: you are under 'digital arrest' for a crime and must stay on camera until verification is complete. It feels official, urgent, and impossible to ignore.
p2: Most people assume arrests never happen over video calls. That’s exactly why this works. Scammers rely on a lesser-known trick: there is no such legal concept as 'digital arrest'. But fear makes people comply before they question.
p3: They keep victims on long calls, often hours, using fake police IDs, court orders, and background noise to build pressure. The goal is isolation and urgency so you don’t verify independently. The safest response is simple: hang up immediately, contact your local police through official numbers, and speak to someone you trust before acting.
conclusion: Have you or someone you know received a 'digital arrest' call, and what made it feel believable or suspicious at the time?
